title: Members should not be more accessible than their containing type
messageFormat: This member is declared public but its containing type is not publicly visible; lower its accessibility.
summary: Marking a member public inside a type that is itself internal or otherwise non-public is misleading: the member can never be reached from outside the assembly because the type that holds it cannot. The wider modifier suggests an external contract that does not exist and hides the member's real reach. Declare the member with accessibility that matches how far it can actually be used.
